Home / MRR Calculator

MRR Calculator

Track your MRR health: new vs churned vs expansion revenue, monthly growth rate, and time to reach key milestones.

$
$

How to Use This Tool

1
Enter your current subscriber count.
2
Enter your monthly price per subscriber.
3
Enter your monthly churn rate as a percentage (e.g. 5 for 5%).
4
Enter expansion MRR from upgrades and add-ons (leave 0 if none).
5
Enter how many new subscribers you typically add per month.
6
Review your net MRR movement — are you growing or shrinking?

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a good MRR growth rate for an entrepreneur?

10-20% month-over-month is excellent for early-stage SaaS. 5-10% is healthy. Below 5% means you need to fix either acquisition or retention. The best entrepreneurs track their net MRR movement (new − churned + expansion) weekly, not monthly.

What churn rate should I target?

Under 3% monthly churn is good for SMB SaaS. Under 1-2% is great. Enterprise SaaS should aim for under 1%. Every percentage point of churn you reduce compounds dramatically — going from 5% to 3% churn can increase customer lifetime by 67%.

How do I increase expansion MRR?

Add usage-based tiers, upsell premium features, offer add-ons (extra seats, storage, integrations), and raise prices for new customers. A healthy SaaS gets 20-30% of new MRR from existing customer expansion, not just new signups.

How accurate are the milestone projections?

They assume constant net change each month — a conservative linear estimate. In reality, growth compounds (if you grow 10% on a larger base, the absolute gain is bigger). Treat these as minimum targets; actual results are usually faster if growth rate holds.

Can I use this for non-SaaS businesses?

Yes. Any business with recurring revenue — memberships, newsletters, coaching retainers — can use MRR as a metric. Just adapt the inputs to your model.

Results

Copy All
Example

📊 MRR Calculation

• Subscribers: 500 • Monthly Price: $29.00 • MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue): $14,500.00 • ARR (Annual Recurring Revenue, no discount): $174,000.00 • ARR (with 20% annual discount): $139,200.00 • Revenue lost to discount: $34,800.00 💡 Annual billing locks in revenue and can reduce churn by 20-40%. The discount is worth it.
